fields.summary: Create a class that covers the functionality of UserGroupMappingPlacementRule and AppNameMappingPlacementRule using the new mapping rules
[Umbrella] Reduce the feature gap between FS Placement Rules and CS Queue Mapping rules

fields.description: As per the design document attached to the umbrella Jira (YARN-10370), we need to replace the current PlacementRule classes with a new generic one, which can utilize the more generic MappingRule approach. This way we can even mix User and Application placement rules and define a custom order between all the rules, not only the rules within the same category (user or applcation placement rules)

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

Hi [~shuzirra],

1. Nit: CSMappingPlacementRule#buildValidationContext can be private.

2. CSMappingPlacementRule#buildValidationContext: What's the point of passing the two fields (immutableVariables, queueManager) to this method? As buildValidationContext is an instance method as well, it can access these fields directly.

3. Still in CSMappingPlacementRule#buildValidationContext: This error message looks / sounds weird: 
{code}
LOG.error("Error initializing placement variables unable to register" +
          " '%default': " + e.getMessage());
{code}

4. Nit: Shouldn't we already have a constant for "%default" somewhere? I cannot find it in the codebase.

5. CSMappingPlacementRule#initialize: It's better to refer to the class name in LOG / exception strings with getClass().getSimpleName().

6. CSMappingPlacementRule#initialize: You can use parameterized logging instead of String concatenation in the LOG.error(..) statement. This way you become more consistent with the rest of the code, as some lines below this there's a parameterized LOG.info call.

7. CSMappingPlacementRule#initialize: When you debug log the rules, I think it's better to add one log statement like: "Printing mapping rules: ", then you can have the forEach-debug print you already added to the code.

8. Code duplication in CSMappingPlacementRule#getSecondaryGroup with UserGroupMappingPlacementRule#getSecondaryGroup: Can you extract a static method and pass the QM instance and the groups instance? Would this make sense or do you have different plans to handle this duplication?

9. Nit: CSMappingPlacementRule#getPlacementForApp: Comment in the beginning looks weird with two spaces indentation from the 2nd line.

10. CSMappingPlacementRule#getPlacementForApp: I think it's better to add log messages, at least for the cases where you return null, for better tracebility.

11. CSMappingPlacementRule#getPlacementForApp: In case REJECT, the info log has the word 'action' twice: "fallback action action".

12. TestCSMappingPlacementRule: Extending Testcase and using @Test annotations is unconventional, please fix.

13. TestCSMappingPlacementRule: There are several fields and methods that can be private: userGroups, createQueueHierarchy, setupEngine, createApp, assertReject, assertPlace, assertNull.

14. Please add messages to asserts in methods: assertReject, assertPlace and assertNull.

15. There are two error logs defined as:
{code}
LOG.error("{} {}", message, e);
{code}
"Intellij complains with: "The formatted log message expects 2 arguments, passed 1."
I guess this is because e is an instance of Exception, so I guess you should use the error method that expects a String + an exception as a parameter like: 
{code}
LOG.error(message, e);
{code}

16. Typos in TestCSMappingPlacementRule#testLegacyPlacementToExistingQueue: "defaule", "charile"

17. Last statement of TestCSMappingPlacementRule#testLegacyPlacementToManagedQueues can fit into one single line.

18. Messages in TestCSMappingPlacementRule#testLegacyPlacementShortReference could be easier to understand if the string prefix would be "Should be placed to default: " instead of the current format without colon.

19. Typo in TestCSMappingPlacementRule#testRuleFallbackHandling: "Charile"

20. Typo in TestCSMappingPlacementRule#testConfigValidation: Variable name: nonExistantStatic should be nonExistentStatic

21. I guess it could be more straightforward to split the testcase TestCSMappingPlacementRule#testConfigValidation into 3 separate testcases. Also, the catch-blocks are empty. Is this how they supposed to be?

Thank you for the feedback [~snemeth] and [~pbacsko], I've addressed the issues you mentioned, also fixed the check style issues.

[~snemeth]:

8. Code duplication in CSMappingPlacementRule#getSecondaryGroup with UserGroupMappingPlacementRule#getSecondaryGroup: Can you extract a static method and pass the QM instance and the groups instance? Would this make sense or do you have different plans to handle this duplication?

I'm planning to remove the UserGroupMappingPlacementRule class altogether, thats why I've just moved this method here. Temporarily we could create a helper static class, but it seems pointless.

[~pbacsko]:

3. {{initialize()}} returns true only if {{mappingRules.size() > 0}}. Can't this be a problem? In theory, a JSON with no rules are valid. Does anything fail if we have no rules?

This is how PlacementRules (or placement rule engines are implemented in YARN and especially in CS), what we call PlacementEngine like the CSMappingPlacementRule is just an implementation of the PlacementRule interface, and there can be multiple PlacementRule implementations configured, if one doesn't return a value (null return) we move onto the next. By returning false if we have 0 rules defined, we can save up some unnecessary calls,  by letting the CS know, that this particular engine has 0 rules configured, which means it would only return null, so it can ignore it. Please see updatePlacementRules in CapacityScheduler for example.

Hi [~shuzirra],
I have only 2 minor comments left, which doesn't require a new patch. Instead, it can be part of a follow-up jira.

1. I meant creating a constant for String "%default" in class CSMappingPlacementRule.
2. Please add messages to asserts in methods: assertReject, assertPlace and assertNull. This was 14th previously.

So for this one: LGTM, +1 (binding)

Hi [~shuzirra],

1. Nit: CSMappingPlacementRule#buildValidationContext can be private.

2. CSMappingPlacementRule#buildValidationContext: What's the point of passing the two fields (immutableVariables, queueManager) to this method? As buildValidationContext is an instance method as well, it can access these fields directly.

3. Still in CSMappingPlacementRule#buildValidationContext: This error message looks / sounds weird: 
{code}
LOG.error("Error initializing placement variables unable to register" +
          " '%default': " + e.getMessage());
{code}

4. Nit: Shouldn't we already have a constant for "%default" somewhere? I cannot find it in the codebase.

5. CSMappingPlacementRule#initialize: It's better to refer to the class name in LOG / exception strings with getClass().getSimpleName().

6. CSMappingPlacementRule#initialize: You can use parameterized logging instead of String concatenation in the LOG.error(..) statement. This way you become more consistent with the rest of the code, as some lines below this there's a parameterized LOG.info call.

7. CSMappingPlacementRule#initialize: When you debug log the rules, I think it's better to add one log statement like: "Printing mapping rules: ", then you can have the forEach-debug print you already added to the code.

8. Code duplication in CSMappingPlacementRule#getSecondaryGroup with UserGroupMappingPlacementRule#getSecondaryGroup: Can you extract a static method and pass the QM instance and the groups instance? Would this make sense or do you have different plans to handle this duplication?

9. Nit: CSMappingPlacementRule#getPlacementForApp: Comment in the beginning looks weird with two spaces indentation from the 2nd line.

10. CSMappingPlacementRule#getPlacementForApp: I think it's better to add log messages, at least for the cases where you return null, for better tracebility.

11. CSMappingPlacementRule#getPlacementForApp: In case REJECT, the info log has the word 'action' twice: "fallback action action".

12. TestCSMappingPlacementRule: Extending Testcase and using @Test annotations is unconventional, please fix.

13. TestCSMappingPlacementRule: There are several fields and methods that can be private: userGroups, createQueueHierarchy, setupEngine, createApp, assertReject, assertPlace, assertNull.

14. Please add messages to asserts in methods: assertReject, assertPlace and assertNull.

15. There are two error logs defined as:
{code}
LOG.error("{} {}", message, e);
{code}
"Intellij complains with: "The formatted log message expects 2 arguments, passed 1."
I guess this is because e is an instance of Exception, so I guess you should use the error method that expects a String + an exception as a parameter like: 
{code}
LOG.error(message, e);
{code}

16. Typos in TestCSMappingPlacementRule#testLegacyPlacementToExistingQueue: "defaule", "charile"

17. Last statement of TestCSMappingPlacementRule#testLegacyPlacementToManagedQueues can fit into one single line.

18. Messages in TestCSMappingPlacementRule#testLegacyPlacementShortReference could be easier to understand if the string prefix would be "Should be placed to default: " instead of the current format without colon.

19. Typo in TestCSMappingPlacementRule#testRuleFallbackHandling: "Charile"

20. Typo in TestCSMappingPlacementRule#testConfigValidation: Variable name: nonExistantStatic should be nonExistentStatic

21. I guess it could be more straightforward to split the testcase TestCSMappingPlacementRule#testConfigValidation into 3 separate testcases. Also, the catch-blocks are empty. Is this how they supposed to be?

Thank you for the feedback [~snemeth] and [~pbacsko], I've addressed the issues you mentioned, also fixed the check style issues.

[~snemeth]:

8. Code duplication in CSMappingPlacementRule#getSecondaryGroup with UserGroupMappingPlacementRule#getSecondaryGroup: Can you extract a static method and pass the QM instance and the groups instance? Would this make sense or do you have different plans to handle this duplication?

I'm planning to remove the UserGroupMappingPlacementRule class altogether, thats why I've just moved this method here. Temporarily we could create a helper static class, but it seems pointless.

[~pbacsko]:

3. {{initialize()}} returns true only if {{mappingRules.size() > 0}}. Can't this be a problem? In theory, a JSON with no rules are valid. Does anything fail if we have no rules?

This is how PlacementRules (or placement rule engines are implemented in YARN and especially in CS), what we call PlacementEngine like the CSMappingPlacementRule is just an implementation of the PlacementRule interface, and there can be multiple PlacementRule implementations configured, if one doesn't return a value (null return) we move onto the next. By returning false if we have 0 rules defined, we can save up some unnecessary calls,  by letting the CS know, that this particular engine has 0 rules configured, which means it would only return null, so it can ignore it. Please see updatePlacementRules in CapacityScheduler for example.
